EMS QUEST:
PITTSBURGH

A top-down EMS RPG. Run a shift in the Steel City — drive code three, work scenes, transport patients, keep your crew alive.
!   This game is for computer only — keyboard & mouse required   !
CODES

HOW TO PLAY

DRIVING THE RIG
▸ Use W to accelerate, S to brake / reverse, A/D to turn.
▸ SPACE = handbrake (sharper stops).
▸ L = lights & sirens. Use them on E0/E1 priority calls — other traffic yields, intersections clear faster.
▸ H = headlights (auto-on at night).
▸ T = open the radio chat. Talk to dispatch or your partner.
▸ P = ask your partner for a hint about the current call.
▸ The GPS arrow (right side) points toward your next waypoint with distance.
▸ The dispatch panel below it shows the active call, priority, and a countdown timer for E0/E1 calls — get there before it expires.
▸ Watch the shift clock (top right). When it hits zero, your shift ends.
▸ Speeding, collisions, and unsafe driving damage your rig (Unit HP). Lose all HP and you're out of service.
▸ Crashing into pedestrians or other vehicles also ends shifts early — drive like a real medic.
▸ E at the destination to exit the rig and become the medic on foot.
RUNNING AN ENCOUNTER
▸ After parking, walk to the patient sprite using WASD.
▸ Press E to begin the encounter — a turn-based scene opens.
▸ You'll see the patient's chief complaint, vitals, and the case dispatch description.
▸ Pick actions from your menu: ASSESS (gather more data), TREAT (interventions), TRANSPORT.
▸ Each turn the patient may improve, stay stable, or deteriorate based on your choices.
▸ Watch your PATIENT NOTES panel (right side) — it tracks treatments given, current vitals, and key findings.
▸ When you're ready, TRANSPORT opens the hospital picker. Match the patient to the right facility:
  • Trauma center for major trauma
  • STEMI / cardiac for active MI
  • Stroke center for stroke (within window)
  • Pediatric hospital for kids
  • Closest ED for routine BLS
▸ Picking the right hospital scores +10 and counts as a save. Wrong hospital costs time and may harm the patient.
▸ CALL ALS if a skill is out of your scope — ALS rolls up to take over.
▸ After transport, you get a debrief with feedback on your assessment, treatments, and hospital choice.
REVIEW ARCADE — STUDY MODE
▸ Where to find it: drive to one of the special buildings — MEDICAL BAY or AMBULANCE REPAIR. Both have arcade cabinets inside.
▸ Park nearby, exit your rig (E), walk through the door at the bottom of the building.
▸ Once inside, walk to the arcade cabinet (the standing red-and-blue machine near the desk) and press E to start a quiz session. Time stops while you're in the quiz.

QUIZ SETTINGS
▸ Question count (1–120 freeform). Locked at 120 in NREMT mode.
▸ Feedback mode: CHECK EACH ANSWER shows you instantly if you got it right, with the explanation. REVIEW AT END hides feedback until you submit (more like a real exam).
▸ BACKGROUND MUSIC on/off — the upbeat loop plays during the quiz unless you mute it here.
▸ NREMT EXAM MODE: simulates the real NREMT cognitive exam — 120 questions, 2-hour countdown, NREMT-blueprint categories only. Auto-finishes early at question 70+ if you maintain ≥80% accuracy.
▸ Categories: pick one or more topic areas (resp, cardiac, trauma, neuro, etc.) or leave on ALL for an even spread.

DURING THE QUIZ
▸ Multi-select questions show a red SELECT N ANSWERS badge at the top.
▸ The flag icon (top right) marks a question for review at the end.
▸ BACK / NEXT walk through questions; SUBMIT NOW ends the session early.
▸ Underlined terms in stems and explanations are glossary entries — hover to see the definition.
▸ Results screen shows overall score, per-category breakdown, and a review of every question with explanations.
▸ Walk back to the arcade door and press E to return to the street.
✚ ✚

CREDITS

This game was vibe coded in Claude
by Michael Bockstaller!

INSPIRATION

The review-style quiz portion of this game was heavily inspired by Pocket Prep, but is not affiliated with nor endorsed by Pocket Prep.

PROTOTYPE NOTICE

This game is a prototype. Expect bugs, edge-case errors, unfinished content, and clinical content that may not perfectly match local protocol. Do not use as a substitute for your protocol manual, official course materials, or your medical director’s guidance.

AUTHOR’S NOTE

I hope you were able to review your EMS content using this tool! :)
Thank you for playing!

TUTORIAL

ACHIEVEMENTS

✚ ✚

BUILD YOUR SHIFT

Pick the role you want to run today and the rig you'll be driving.
YOUR ROLE
VEHICLE
PREVIEW
Pick a role and a rig to see them in action.
Set your shift length, narrow the call mix, and pick how dispatch talks on the radio.
SHIFT LENGTH (1 game hour = 1 real minute)
TYPES OF EMERGENCY (filter call types — pick one or more)
RADIO STYLE
Off: plain language ("acknowledged", "en route") — the modern NIMS standard.
On: classic 10-codes ("10-4", "10-23", "10-8") — still used by some legacy services.

RADIO 10-CODES — PA EMS

Most modern PA EMS agencies have moved to plain-language per NIMS for inter-agency interoperability. But some legacy services, college EMS units, and police channels still use 10-codes. Here are the ones you'll hear most.
10-3 Stop transmitting / clarify your last
10-4 Acknowledged / received / understood
10-7 Out of service / unavailable
10-8 In service / available for calls / back in quarters
10-9 Repeat / say again last
10-12 Stand by / hold
10-17 Urgent / pick up message
10-19 Returning to quarters / return to base
10-20 Your location ("what's your 20?")
10-22 Disregard / cancel last
10-23 Arrived on scene / on location
10-33 Emergency / officer or crew needs assistance
10-50 Motor vehicle accident
10-76 En route / responding
Signal 13 PGH-specific: emergency assistance / officer down

COMMON ABBREVIATIONS

ETA Estimated time of arrival
RP Reporting party (caller)
Pt Patient
A&Ox4 Alert & oriented to person, place, time, event
LKW / LSW Last known well / last seen well (stroke)
LZ Landing zone (helicopter)
ETOH Alcohol (intoxication)
RMA / AMA Refused medical attention / against medical advice
PPCI Primary percutaneous coronary intervention (cath lab)
QRS / QRV Quick response squad / vehicle (non-transport unit)
Note: PA Department of Health and most regional EMS councils encourage plain-language radio traffic for inter-agency clarity. 10-codes vary between agencies — a 10-50 might mean different things to police vs EMS vs fire. When in doubt, use plain English on the air.
